title: "Ever Played Soccer That Actually Feels Good?" — ASMR Swipe-to-Score
hook_3s: A finger drags a glowing trajectory line across a sunny pitch — "Ever played a soccer game that actually feels this satisfying?"
scene_flow: 0-3s: Close-up of a thumb swiping on screen; a bright green trajectory trail curves around two stickman defenders. Soft ASMR swipe sound. Overlay question hooks the viewer.
3-7s: Camera follows the ball as it threads the gap, ping-ponging off a defender's shin with a juicy physics bounce. Level tracker ticks 1→2→3 in rapid succession.
7-11s: Montage of 3 satisfying goals — top-corner net ripple, keeper faceplant, ball spinning into slow-mo. Each goal pops a green +1 confetti burst.
11-14s: Level counter zooms to 27, trajectory trails layer into a mesmerizing pattern across the sunny yellow pitch.
14-15s: End card: ball rolls into goal, net says 'CRAZY KICK!' — CTA button pulses.
visual_direction: Lean fully into Crazy Kick's signature sandy-yellow pitch (#F5D77F) and sky-blue stadium backdrop (#1CB4F0), with the bright green (#99CC33) reserved for the trajectory trail, level-up confetti, and CTA — making "satisfaction" visually legible through color rhythm, not stylus-puzzle calm. Keep the 3D hyper-casual low-poly stickman aesthetic intact: chunky defenders, exaggerated ball physics, clean shadowless lighting. The "ASMR satisfaction" feeling comes from slow-mo ball spin, net ripples, and the rhythmic stacking of trajectory trails — never from puzzle-sliding visuals.
text_overlays: Ever played soccer that feels THIS good?
rationale: Voodoo's current Crazy Kick rotation leans hard on "insane street football" energy and generic playable/video CTAs — none tap the trending "satisfaction/ASMR question-hook" format that's winning across casual right now. This brief imports that proven hook structure ("Ever played a game that actually feels…?") but honestly delivers Crazy Kick's real swipe-to-aim physics, avoiding the bus-jam bait-and-switch policy risk flagged in game-fit. UA team: test against the Youtube "street football glory" creative to isolate hook-style lift on IPM.
scenario_prompts: Vertical 9:16 mobile game screenshot, authentic in-game moment from Crazy Kick soccer game, 3D hyper-casual low-poly bright and colorful with simple stickman characters, sandy yellow pitch (#F5D77F), sky blue stadium background (#1CB4F0), glowing bright green (#99CC33) curved trajectory trail arcing from foreground around two low-poly stickman defenders toward a goal, white soccer ball mid-flight with motion blur, top-down-angled action camera, level progression tracker '27' visible top-center in chunky rounded HUD, large white sans-serif overlay text 'Ever played soccer that feels THIS good?' at top, finger silhouette swiping at bottom of screen, no extra invented characters, clean shadowless lighting, hero frame

Vertical 9:16 mobile game screenshot, authentic Crazy Kick gameplay frame, 3D hyper-casual low-poly bright and colorful with simple stickman characters, sandy yellow field (#F5D77F) with sky blue (#1CB4F0) crowd stands, low-poly stickman goalkeeper diving the wrong way, white ball rippling into the net with bright green (#99CC33) confetti burst, level tracker advancing '12 → 13' in HUD, big bold overlay text '…and watch this 👀' centered, energetic competitive UI mood, no stylus or puzzle elements, storyboard frame
